educations.extra: Member of the programming team for two years and captain for one

company.name: Epic
company.description: Electronic Health Record system with over 190 million patients worldwide

teams.name: ICU
projects: Created the infrastructure that allows generically integrating any video vendor's product with Epic's remote monitoring application. Previously these integrations had to be done manually and were thus extremely cost prohibitive.

teams.name: Special Projects (aka Skunkworks)
teams.description: Task force that meant to advance framework and improve developer retention
projects: Updated the web framework to support something akin to "hot reloading," whereby developers would be able to see their UI changes in near realtime.
Improved Epic's integration with version control tools, saving time and reducing user error.
Rewrote key parts of the core web framework to be immutable, dramatically improving performance.
Helped introduce React Functional Components to the existing architecture, which has helped foster increased reusability of components and, anecdotally, developer satisfaction.

teams.name: Social Care
projects: Created an interactive graphic of a patient's support network. It's being used today to ensure patients are properly cared for ouside of the medical setting.

teams.name: Telehealth
projects: Lead architect on flagship product that allows for organizations to pool resources for virtual care. This improved availability of these services, reduced patient wait times, and reduced unnecessary usage of in-person facilities.

teams.name: Endocrinology
projects: Part of small team that pioneered Epic's first Endocrinology module.
